text: A can complete 3/4th work of B in 5/6 time than B. If the whole work is completed in 10 days by working together, then A alone can complete the work in how many days?
explanation: A's rate = (3/4) / (5/6) = 9/10 of B's rate. So rate A:B = 9:10. With both finishing in 10 days, B alone takes 19 days and A alone takes 190/9 ~ 21 days (check: 9/190 + 1/19 = 19/190 = 1/10).

text: Two taps A and B can fill a tank in 48 minutes and 36 minutes respectively. If both taps are opened together, after how much time should tap A be closed so that the tank fills in 25 minutes 30 seconds?
explanation: B runs the full 25.5 min: 25.5/36 = 0.7083 of the tank. A must fill the rest 0.2917, so t/48 = 0.2917, t = 14 minutes.

text: If a man had walked 20 km/hr faster, he would have saved 1 hour over a distance of 600 km. What is his usual speed?
A batsman scores 87 runs in his 17th innings and thus increases his average by 3 runs. Find his current average.
explanation: Let new average = A. 16(A-3) + 87 = 17A gives 39 = A. Current average = 39 runs.

text: The price of sugar is increased by 30%. Because of this, a family uses 40 kg less sugar, yet its expenditure increases by 10%. Find the original consumption.

A dog chases a rabbit. The dog takes 6 leaps for every 7 leaps of the rabbit, and the distance covered by the rabbit in 6 leaps equals the distance covered by the dog in 5 leaps. Find the ratio of their speeds (dog : rabbit).
explanation: Let dog leap = d, rabbit leap = r. 6r = 5d so r = 5d/6. Speed dog = 6d, speed rabbit = 7r = 35d/6. Ratio = 6d : 35d/6 = 36 : 35.

text: A and B start a business with Rs. 16000 and Rs. 15000 respectively. After 3 months, A withdraws Rs. 5000 and B invests Rs. 5000 more. After a further 3 months, C joins with Rs. 21000. If the total annual profit is Rs. 24900, find the share of C.
Two numbers A and B are such that their geometric mean is 20% lower than their arithmetic mean. Find the ratio between the numbers.
explanation: GM = 0.8 AM, so sqrt(ab) = 0.4(a+b). With a = kb, sqrt(k) = 0.4(k+1) gives k = 4. Ratio = 4:1.

text: Two balls are to be drawn from a bag containing eight grey and three blue balls. Find the probability that both will be blue.
Two fair dice are thrown. Given that the sum of the dice is less than or equal to four, find the probability that only one die shows two.

Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. Which of the following is WRONG?
options: Betsy is to the immediate left of Herbert
Herbert is to the immediate left of Edward
Dennis is 4th to the right of Fiona
explanation: Clockwise order (= left): Dennis, Chloe, Edward, Herbert, Betsy, Fiona, Giles, Alex. Dennis is 4th to the right (anticlockwise) of Fiona would land on Chloe, not Dennis, so statement (C) is wrong.

text: Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. Which of the following is correct?
options: Dennis is to the immediate left of Giles
Alex is between Chloe and Dennis
Fiona is 3rd to the right of Dennis
Edward is between Herbert and Betsy
explanation: Order clockwise: Dennis, Chloe, Edward, Herbert, Betsy, Fiona, Giles, Alex. Going 3rd to the right (anticlockwise) of Dennis: Alex, Giles, Fiona -> Fiona. So (C) is correct.

text: Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. Which group has the 2nd person sitting between the 1st and the 3rd?
explanation: Alex sits directly between Giles and Dennis in the arrangement (Giles, Alex, Dennis are consecutive), so (B) is correct.

text: Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. Which pair has the 1st person sitting to the immediate right of the second?
explanation: Alex is the immediate right (anticlockwise) neighbour of Dennis, so (D) is correct.

text: Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. Which pair are fourth (directly opposite) to one another?
explanation: Opposite pairs (4 seats apart) are Dennis-Betsy, Chloe-Fiona, Edward-Giles, Herbert-Alex. So Dennis-Betsy (D) is correct.

text: Read the following and answer. Eight gamblers - Alex, Betsy, Chloe, Dennis, Edward, Fiona, Giles and Herbert - sit around a round table facing the centre. (i) Dennis is a neighbour of Alex but not of Herbert. (ii) Betsy is a neighbour of Fiona and 4th to the left of Dennis. (iii) Edward is a neighbour of Herbert and 3rd to the right of Fiona. (iv) Chloe is neither a neighbour of Alex nor of Giles. If Chloe and Giles interchange their positions, which of the following indicates Alex's position?
options: To the immediate right of Giles
To the immediate left of Chloe
explanation: After the swap, Chloe takes Giles's old seat, whose left (clockwise) neighbour is Alex. So Alex is to the immediate left of Chloe (D).

text: Read the following and answer. Five cities - Aurangabad, Ahmednagar, Pune, Mumbai and Nagpur - each got more rain than usual. They lie in the mountains, the forest, the coast, the desert and a valley. The rainfall amounts were 24, 54, 64, 88 and 130 inches. (i) The desert city got the least rain; the forest city got the most. (ii) Pune is in the mountains. (iii) Aurangabad got more rain than Mumbai. (iv) Ahmednagar got more than Nagpur but less than Pune. (v) Mumbai got 88 inches; the coast city got 54 inches. (vi) The mountains city got 64 inches. Which city is in the desert?
explanation: Pune=mountains=64, Mumbai=valley=88, Aurangabad=forest=130, Ahmednagar=coast=54, Nagpur=desert=24. The desert city is Nagpur.

text: Read the following and answer. Five cities - Aurangabad, Ahmednagar, Pune, Mumbai and Nagpur - each got more rain than usual. They lie in the mountains, the forest, the coast, the desert and a valley. The rainfall amounts were 24, 54, 64, 88 and 130 inches. (i) The desert city got the least rain; the forest city got the most. (ii) Pune is in the mountains. (iii) Aurangabad got more rain than Mumbai. (iv) Ahmednagar got more than Nagpur but less than Pune. (v) Mumbai got 88 inches; the coast city got 54 inches. (vi) The mountains city got 64 inches. Which city got the most rain?
explanation: The forest city got the most (130 inches), which is Aurangabad.

text: Read the following and answer. Five cities - Aurangabad, Ahmednagar, Pune, Mumbai and Nagpur - each got more rain than usual. They lie in the mountains, the forest, the coast, the desert and a valley. The rainfall amounts were 24, 54, 64, 88 and 130 inches. (i) The desert city got the least rain; the forest city got the most. (ii) Pune is in the mountains. (iii) Aurangabad got more rain than Mumbai. (iv) Ahmednagar got more than Nagpur but less than Pune. (v) Mumbai got 88 inches; the coast city got 54 inches. (vi) The mountains city got 64 inches. How much rain did Ahmednagar get?
explanation: Ahmednagar is the coast city, which got 54 inches.

text: Read the following and answer. Five cities - Aurangabad, Ahmednagar, Pune, Mumbai and Nagpur - each got more rain than usual. They lie in the mountains, the forest, the coast, the desert and a valley. The rainfall amounts were 24, 54, 64, 88 and 130 inches. (i) The desert city got the least rain; the forest city got the most. (ii) Pune is in the mountains. (iii) Aurangabad got more rain than Mumbai. (iv) Ahmednagar got more than Nagpur but less than Pune. (v) Mumbai got 88 inches; the coast city got 54 inches. (vi) The mountains city got 64 inches. Where is Mumbai located?
explanation: Mumbai got 88 inches, which corresponds to the valley.
